aliases.name: Set
aliases.comment: Component storage, main building block of world

aliases.name: Spec
aliases.comment: Component specification, how to get `Component.Set` from and world, and set back into world (used by Systems)
aliases.type: { get : world -> Logic.Component.Set comp, set : Logic.Component.Set comp -> world -> world }

values.name: empty
values.comment: Initial value of any empty `Component.Set` - mostly used to init component sets in world
values.type: Logic.Component.Set comp

aliases.name: Spec
aliases.comment: Main way of creating ECS "anti-pattern", but time to time you need to create some component that can exist only one per whole application (viewport, keyboard, websocket-connection, etc. )
aliases.type: { get : world -> comp, set : comp -> world -> world }

values.name: update
values.comment: Update whole component set, can be used also with `Component.Set` (not only `Singleton`)
values.type: { get : world -> comp, set : comp -> world -> world } -> (comp -> comp) -> world -> world

name: Logic.Entity

values.name: create
values.comment:
Start point for spawning entity

    Entity.create ( id, world )
        |> Entity.with ( positionSpec, positionComponent )
        |> Entity.with ( velocitySpec, velocityComponent )
values.type: Logic.Entity.EntityID -> world -> ( Logic.Entity.EntityID, world )

values.name: fromDict
values.type: Dict.Dict Logic.Entity.EntityID a -> Logic.Component.Set a

values.name: fromList
values.type: List.List ( Logic.Entity.EntityID, a ) -> Logic.Component.Set a

values.name: getComponent
values.type: Basics.Int -> Logic.Component.Set comp -> Maybe.Maybe comp

values.name: mapComponent
values.type: (Maybe.Maybe comp -> Maybe.Maybe comp) -> Logic.Entity.EntityID -> Logic.Component.Set comp -> Logic.Component.Set comp

values.name: mapComponentSet
values.type: (comp -> comp) -> Logic.Entity.EntityID -> Logic.Component.Set comp -> Logic.Component.Set comp

values.name: removeComponent
values.comment: Remove component from `Component.Set` by `EntityID` return new `Component.Set`, or return unchanged if component not in set
values.type: Logic.Entity.EntityID -> Logic.Component.Set a -> Logic.Component.Set a

values.name: removeFor
values.comment:
Way to create `Entity` destruction functions, should pipe in all possible component sets, from what `Entity` should be removed.
Also can be used to just disable (remove) some components from entity

    remove =
        Entity.removeFor positionSpec
            >> Entity.removeFor velocitySpec

    newWorld =
        remove ( id, world )
values.type: Logic.Component.Spec comp world -> ( Logic.Entity.EntityID, world ) -> ( Logic.Entity.EntityID, world )

values.name: setComponent
values.comment: Set the component at a particular index. Returns an updated component Set. If the index is out of range, the Set is unaltered.
values.type: Logic.Entity.EntityID -> a -> Logic.Component.Set a -> Logic.Component.Set a

values.name: spawnComponent
values.comment: Safe way to create component, same as setComponent, only if index is out of range Component.Set will be stretched\\
values.type: Logic.Entity.EntityID -> a -> Logic.Component.Set a -> Logic.Component.Set a

values.name: toDict
values.type: Logic.Component.Set a -> Dict.Dict Logic.Entity.EntityID a

values.name: toList
values.type: Logic.Component.Set a -> List.List ( Logic.Entity.EntityID, a )

values.name: with
values.comment:
Setting components to spawn with new entity

    Entity.create ( id, world )
        |> Entity.with ( positionSpec, positionComponent )
        |> Entity.with ( velocitySpec, velocityComponent )
values.type: ( Logic.Component.Spec comp world, comp ) -> ( Logic.Entity.EntityID, world ) -> ( Logic.Entity.EntityID, world )

unions.name: GameFlow
unions.comment:
game flow for update function

1.  `Running` - runs in normal way 60FPS
2.  `Pause` - game is stopped, and no `Systems` is running
3.  `SlowMotion { frames = 60, fps = 20 }` - will run game **20FPS** for next **60** frames (3s)

aliases.name: Model
aliases.comment: Extendable Model to run inside update
aliases.type: { a | frame : Basics.Int, runtime_ : Basics.Float, flow : Logic.GameFlow.GameFlow }

values.name: update
values.type: (Logic.GameFlow.Model a -> Logic.GameFlow.Model a) -> Basics.Float -> Logic.GameFlow.Model a -> Logic.GameFlow.Model a

values.name: updateWith
values.comment: same as update, but instead of Model, takes tuple, to make same output as default Elm update function
values.type: (Logic.GameFlow.ExtendedModel a b -> Logic.GameFlow.ExtendedModel a b) -> Basics.Float -> Logic.GameFlow.ExtendedModel a b -> Logic.GameFlow.ExtendedModel a b

name: Logic.Launcher
comment:
@docs document
@docs Document, Error, Launcher, World, task, worker

unions.name: Error
unions.comment: Error type to hold all error of game init

aliases.name: Document
aliases.comment:
difference from `Browser.document`:

1.  `init` takes as argument flags and returns Task, that results in world or fail (90% of games any way need get some data to start, images, level data, connection, etc.)
2.  `subscriptions` instead of returning msg - should return new `World`
3.  `update` have no `Msg` input
4.  `view` instead of `Msg` event have to return function
aliases.type: { init : flags -> Task.Task Logic.Launcher.Error (Logic.Launcher.World world), subscriptions : Logic.Launcher.World world -> Platform.Sub.Sub (Logic.Launcher.World world), update : Logic.Launcher.World world -> ( Logic.Launcher.World world, Platform.Cmd.Cmd (Logic.Launcher.Message world) ), view : Logic.Launcher.World world -> List.List (Html.Html (Logic.Launcher.World world -> Logic.Launcher.World world)) }

values.name: document
values.comment: Main entry point, but have few difference from `Browser.document`
values.type: Logic.Launcher.Document flags world -> Logic.Launcher.Launcher flags world

values.name: task
values.comment: Helper function, that allow convert `Task` into Cmd - for loading new `World` after `init`
values.type: (Result.Result x a -> Logic.Launcher.World world -> Logic.Launcher.World world) -> Task.Task x a -> Platform.Cmd.Cmd (Logic.Launcher.Message world)

values.name: worker
values.comment: Main entry point, but have few difference from `Platform.worker`
values.type: { init : flags -> Task.Task Logic.Launcher.Error (Logic.Launcher.World world), subscriptions : Logic.Launcher.World world -> Platform.Sub.Sub (Logic.Launcher.World world), update : Logic.Launcher.World world -> ( Logic.Launcher.World world, Platform.Cmd.Cmd (Logic.Launcher.Message world) ) } -> Logic.Launcher.Launcher flags world

name: Logic.System

aliases.name: SetsReducer2
aliases.type: ( a, a -> acc -> acc ) -> ( b, b -> acc -> acc ) -> acc -> acc

aliases.name: SetsReducer3
aliases.type: ( a, a -> acc -> acc ) -> ( b, b -> acc -> acc ) -> ( c, c -> acc -> acc ) -> acc -> acc

aliases.name: SetsReducer4
aliases.type: ( a, a -> acc -> acc ) -> ( b, b -> acc -> acc ) -> ( c, c -> acc -> acc ) -> ( d, d -> acc -> acc ) -> acc -> acc

values.name: andMap
values.type: Logic.Component.Spec comp world -> Logic.System.UnfinishedSystem world acc ( Logic.Component.Set comp, next ) (( comp, comp -> acc -> acc ) -> newFunc) -> Logic.System.UnfinishedSystem world acc next newFunc

values.name: applyIf
values.type: Basics.Bool -> (a -> a) -> a -> a

values.name: end
values.type: Logic.System.UnfinishedSystem world acc () (acc -> acc) -> world

values.name: endCustom
values.type: custom -> Logic.System.UnfinishedSystem world acc () (( acc, custom ) -> ( acc, custom )) -> ( world, custom )

values.name: foldl
values.type: (comp1 -> acc -> acc) -> Logic.Component.Set comp1 -> acc -> acc

values.name: foldl2
values.type: (comp1 -> comp2 -> acc -> acc) -> Logic.Component.Set comp1 -> Logic.Component.Set comp2 -> acc -> acc

values.name: foldl3
values.type: (comp1 -> comp2 -> comp3 -> acc -> acc) -> Logic.Component.Set comp1 -> Logic.Component.Set comp2 -> Logic.Component.Set comp3 -> acc -> acc

values.name: foldl4
values.type: (comp1 -> comp2 -> comp3 -> comp4 -> acc -> acc) -> Logic.Component.Set comp1 -> Logic.Component.Set comp2 -> Logic.Component.Set comp3 -> Logic.Component.Set comp4 -> acc -> acc

values.name: indexedFoldl
values.type: (Logic.Entity.EntityID -> comp1 -> acc -> acc) -> Logic.Component.Set comp1 -> acc -> acc

values.name: indexedFoldl2
values.type: (Basics.Int -> comp1 -> comp2 -> acc -> acc) -> Logic.Component.Set comp1 -> Logic.Component.Set comp2 -> acc -> acc

values.name: indexedFoldl3
values.type: (Logic.Entity.EntityID -> comp1 -> comp2 -> comp3 -> acc -> acc) -> Logic.Component.Set comp1 -> Logic.Component.Set comp2 -> Logic.Component.Set comp3 -> acc -> acc

values.name: start
values.type: (( comp, acc -> ( Array.Array (Maybe.Maybe acc), next ) -> ( Array.Array (Maybe.Maybe acc), next ) ) -> func) -> Logic.Component.Spec comp world -> world -> Logic.System.UnfinishedSystem world ( Logic.Component.Set comp, next ) next func

values.name: step
values.comment: single component mapping, same as List.map - only for `Component.Set` inside `World`
values.type: (comp -> comp) -> Logic.Component.Spec comp world -> world -> world

values.name: step2
values.comment:
Example:

    system : Logic.Component.Spec Velocity world -> Logic.Component.Spec Position world -> System world
       system =
           Logic.System.step2
               (\( velocity, _ ) ( pos, setPos ) -> setPos (Vec2.add velocity pos))
values.type: Logic.System.SetsReducer2 a b { a : Logic.Component.Set a, b : Logic.Component.Set b } -> Logic.Component.Spec a world -> Logic.Component.Spec b world -> world -> world

values.name: step3
values.type: Logic.System.SetsReducer3 a b c { a : Logic.Component.Set a, b : Logic.Component.Set b, c : Logic.Component.Set c } -> Logic.Component.Spec a world -> Logic.Component.Spec b world -> Logic.Component.Spec c world -> world -> world

values.name: step4
values.type: Logic.System.SetsReducer4 a b c d { a : Logic.Component.Set a, b : Logic.Component.Set b, c : Logic.Component.Set c, d : Logic.Component.Set d } -> Logic.Component.Spec a world -> Logic.Component.Spec b world -> Logic.Component.Spec c world -> Logic.Component.Spec d world -> world -> world
